title: This Former DeepMind Exec Thinks the AI Arms Race Could End in Disaster
gist: Former Google DeepMind policy head Verity Harding argues the 'AI arms race' metaphor is self-fulfilling and dangerous, forecloses international cooperation, and accrues power to major labs; she proposes a 'middle powers coalition' and warns the current path leads to centralized government control and vassal states.

insights.claim: Casting AI as a lethal weapon risks closing off the international cooperation needed to make it safe and to distribute its benefits, and forces smaller powers to line up behind one superpower against their own interests.
insights.speaker: Joel Khalili
insights.so_what: Frames a policy fork that shapes export controls, alliances, and where AI capital and compute flow.
insights.locator: Harding believes that casting AI as a lethal weapon

insights.claim: Believing the AI arms race framing makes it true, turning smaller countries into interchangeable chess pieces on one superpower's side rather than independent actors.
insights.speaker: Joel Khalili
insights.so_what: Narrative itself is treated as a causal driver of geopolitical alignment, not just commentary.
insights.quote: By believing it to be true, you make it true, turning yourself into a small chess piece on one side or the other. You are always lesser.
insights.locator: The point is to not allow the arms race framing

insights.claim: AI research was rooted in international cooperation as recently as 2016-2020 before shifting toward a framing of civilizational battle between the West and China.
insights.speaker: Joel Khalili
insights.so_what: Dates the cooperation-to-rivalry inflection, useful for gauging how entrenched the current stance is.
insights.quote: AI research “was rooted in international cooperation.”
insights.locator: As the head of global public policy at Google DeepMind

themes.theme: Forces and triggers behind the shift

insights.claim: Two forces drove the arms-race narrative: a sincere belief that dangerous AI means democracies should hold the keys, and an anti-regulation stream that finds China useful as a bogeyman to argue 'if you regulate us, you let China win.'
insights.speaker: Joel Khalili
insights.so_what: Identifies who benefits from the framing, including firms using it to deflect regulation.
insights.quote: “If you regulate us, you let China win.”
insights.locator: What were the forces behind that shift?

insights.claim: ChatGPT's November 2022 launch, coinciding with the pandemic's reversal of a borderless world and the Ukraine war making AI weaponry feel real, rapidly cemented AI as 'the new arms race' mapped onto the Cold War.
insights.speaker: Joel Khalili
insights.so_what: Pinpoints the confluence of events that hardened the nuclear-analogy framing.
insights.quote: It very quickly became accepted wisdom that AI is the new arms race.
insights.locator: ChatGPT emerged at the same time as a global pandemic

insights.claim: Major AI labs are complicit in shifting rhetoric from collaboration to competition because arms-race language accrues power to them by implying only they are equipped to control the solution.
insights.speaker: Joel Khalili
insights.so_what: Suggests incumbent labs have an incentive to sustain the narrative that entrenches their moat.
insights.quote: Talking about AI as an arms race accrues power to them, by saying it's so powerful, new, and unique, that only we have the answer, and only we should be in charge of the solution.
insights.locator: Do you think the major labs are complicit

themes.theme: US policy and Anthropic model withdrawal

insights.claim: Harding reads the Trump administration's nationalist AI rhetoric and its bid to impose export controls on homegrown models as symptoms of the arms-race framing and evidence a worst-case scenario is forming.
insights.speaker: Joel Khalili
insights.so_what: Signals policy risk of tightening US controls even on domestic frontier models.
insights.locator: Harding sees the Trump administration's nationalist AI rhetoric

insights.claim: Within a recent two-week window Trump issued an executive order on AI with nationalist rhetoric and his administration effectively forced Anthropic to withdraw its latest frontier model from the market.
insights.speaker: Joel Khalili
insights.so_what: Concrete regulatory intervention pulling a frontier model shows direct political impact on AI supply.
insights.quote: his administration effectively forced Anthropic to withdraw its latest frontier model from the market.
insights.locator: In the last two weeks, Donald Trump issued an executive order

insights.claim: Even the US and China cannot develop everything alone, creating strategic chokepoints over chips, critical minerals, and scientists, making a fully sovereign national AI stack unrealistic for any country.
insights.speaker: Joel Khalili
insights.so_what: Chokepoint interdependence argues against clean decoupling and highlights leverage points in the AI supply chain.
insights.quote: It's unrealistic to suggest that every country can have their own completely sovereign AI stack.
insights.locator: Even the US and China can't develop everything themselves

insights.claim: Harding proposes a middle powers coalition—e.g. Canada, France, Japan, South Korea, India, and the UK—combining India's scale and diffusion, the UK's talent and startup ecosystem, and Canada's critical minerals for leverage and scale.
insights.speaker: Joel Khalili
insights.so_what: Identifies a potential third bloc that could reshape AI alliances and resource bargaining.
insights.quote: I've been calling for a middle powers coalition.
insights.locator: But the internationalist approach isn't in opposition

insights.claim: Continuing down the arms-race road leads to excessive government control and centralization over AI systems, less safe and beneficial systems, and many vassal states forced to pick a superpower.
insights.speaker: Joel Khalili
insights.so_what: A bearish long-run scenario for open, distributed AI development and independent national tech policy.
insights.quote: The natural long-term conclusion is excessive government control and centralization of power over these systems
insights.locator: So, if we continue down this road
